sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Real Betis have shown consistency with no losses in their last 3 matches, securing 1 win and 2 draws. Athletic Club are in strong form with 2 wins in their last 2 league games, scoring 4 goals. Both teams have demonstrated defensive solidity with few goals conceded recently.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: The last two encounters between Real Betis and Athletic Club ended in draws (2-2 and 1-1), indicating tight matches. Both teams scored in each of these games, suggesting a pattern of competitive and goal-filled contests.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Real Betis rank 7th with an overperformance in expected goals (+1.14) and strong chance creation (2nd in big chances created). Athletic Club, positioned 4th, have a potent attack (4 goals in 2 games) and tend to be involved in high-scoring matches (6/7 over 2.5 goals). Both teams average around 4 corners per game.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Tactical Insights
sections.text: With the referee averaging 5.9 yellow cards per game and head-to-head stats showing frequent cards (8/10 matches over 4.5 cards), expect a physical game. Both teams' recent matches often see fewer than 10.5 corners, aligning with their season averages.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the scoreline remains goalless between Real Betis and Athletic Club at the Estadio de la Cartuja in Seville. Neither team has managed to break the deadlock despite some promising moments. Both sides have shown intent, with fairly balanced possession and several entries into the final third. However, the lack of clinical finishing has kept the game level at the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Real Betis have held a slight edge in ball possession and have been competitive in duels, winning a notable percentage of both ground and aerial challenges. They've had a few entries into the penalty area and even hit the woodwork once, indicating they’ve been close to scoring. On the other hand, Athletic Club have looked more threatening in front of goal, creating a big chance and getting more shots on target. They've also had more penalty area entries and won possession in dangerous areas, suggesting a sharper attacking edge despite missing a key opportunity.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two teams. Real Betis started strongly, dictating the early stages with consistent pressure in the opposition half. However, Athletic Club responded with a significant shift in momentum, taking control for a prolonged period with several dangerous attacking spells. Towards the latter part of the half, Betis regained some initiative, pushing forward with intent, but Athletic Club countered with late surges, keeping the home defense on their toes. The back-and-forth nature of the attacks suggests both teams are capable of creating decisive moments.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Real Betis, Cucho Hernández and Giovani Lo Celso have shown glimpses of creativity and could be pivotal in unlocking Athletic Club’s defense in the second half. Their ability to link play in the final third will be crucial. For Athletic Club, Iñaki Williams stands out as a major threat, having been heavily involved in their attacking plays and coming close to scoring. His pace and positioning could prove decisive if given space.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Real Betis have relied on a structured approach, maintaining possession and looking to build through the middle, though they’ve lacked the final touch in the box. Their defense, led by Marc Bartra, has been solid with several clearances. Athletic Club, meanwhile, have been more direct, utilizing width and quick transitions to create chances. However, their finishing has let them down, and a yellow card early in the game hints at potential disciplinary issues if Betis apply pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see a more open game as both teams push for a breakthrough. Athletic Club may capitalize on their attacking threat, especially through quick counters, while Real Betis will look to convert their possession into more meaningful opportunities. Defensive discipline will be key for both sides, but the away team’s higher shot count in the first half suggests they might have the upper hand if they sharpen up in front of goal.
